what_it_does: Stores a structured interpretive layer for the AUDIT-INDEX.md of the claude-ds project: a JSON record that restates the audit's purpose, lists observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, questions, themes, inferred intentions, novelty, contribution, and embedding-ready summaries, with no code symbols.
observations: A JSON object with project/path/model metadata and a 'what_it_does' summary of the underlying AUDIT-INDEX.md.
Contains an 'observations' array describing the audit's content-type map, ten reconciliation tensions, KEEP markers, vigilance notes, and next step.
Includes 'standouts', 'conventions', 'concerns', 'notes', and 'questions' fields evaluating the audit itself.
References companion files such as REQUIREMENTS.md, AXES.md, DIAGRAMS.md, and SYSTEM-GAPS.md as context for the tensions.
standouts: The file is a meta-interpretation: a JSON layer describing an audit index that itself maps and diagnoses a design system.
It preserves the audit's governing authority rule — source material is authoritative for DNA, v1 docs are authoritative for what was built.
The tension format 'what v1 says → what source shows → resolution' plus KEEP markers is carried through as a structured reconciliation ledger.
Embedding fields ('summary_for_embedding', 'intention_for_embedding') are provided for downstream retrieval.
conventions: Snake_case keys and string-array values for observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, questions, and themes.
Neutral, descriptive language without ranking or prescriptive judgments.
Embedding-ready one-sentence summaries at the end.
Explicit empty symbols list when no code symbols are present.
References to companion docs are kept as pointers rather than restatements.
concerns: The file's value depends on the companion docs it names; read alone it cannot resolve the tensions.
Line counts and file references copied from the audit may drift if the underlying markdown changes.
No timestamp, hash, or provenance field indicates when this interpretation was generated or against which version of the source.
notes: A JSON interpretive snapshot of the claude-ds AUDIT-INDEX.md, packaging its audit map, tensions, resolutions, and DNA-preservation signals for machine retrieval.
questions: Was this JSON generated automatically from the markdown, or curated, and at what point in time?
What do the 'opus' model tag and 'v2-full' prompt_version signify for this interpretation?
Are the cited line counts and companion-file references still synchronized with the current source files?
How are the 'owning slices' in the content-type map identified and assigned?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file presents itself as a structured interpretive record of the named audit index and delivers the expected metadata, observations, themes, and embedding summaries.
intention_signals: Inferred: to turn the AUDIT-INDEX.md audit into a queryable, machine-readable diagnostic record so a fused system can locate the design system's content-type map, tensions, resolutions, and preserved DNA without re-reading the original markdown.
novelty: Distinct as a JSON interpretive wrapper around an audit index, with explicit embedding summaries and a declared empty symbol set, rather than the audit document itself.
contribution: Contributes a structured, retrievable interpretation of the audit index — including its authority rule, content-type map, ten tensions with resolutions, KEEP markers, and embedding summaries — that a fused system can keep and query.
summary_for_embedding: JSON interpretive layer for the claude-ds AUDIT-INDEX.md, capturing the whole-system content-type map, ten v1-docs-vs-source-DNA reconciliation tensions with resolutions and KEEP markers, vigilance notes, and embedding-ready summaries.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to make the audit index's diagnostic ledger — content types, tensions, resolutions, and DNA to preserve — searchable and usable by a fused system independently of the original markdown.
